summary: Regenerated the raw assembly slice for the menu character-select module, asm/melee/mn/mncharsel.s. The diff is almost entirely a mechanical address/label relocation pass: file range comments changed from section-relative offsets like 0x00000000-0x0000B31C to DOL/runtime addresses 0x8025BC20-0x80266F3C, local labels changed from .L_000xxxxx to .L_802xxxxx, and instruction comments/relocations gained concrete addresses and branch/call displacements.

key_files.role: Only changed file; regenerated assembly for mnCharSel functions, replacing zero-based disassembly offsets with runtime addresses and updated labels/relocations.

what_changed: Updated the assembly file header range from '# 0x00000000 - 0x0000B31C' to '# 0x8025BC20 - 0x80266F3C'.
Rewrote instruction address comments from section-relative pairs such as '/* 00000000 00000040 */' to concrete text/DOL-address comments such as '/* 8025BC20 00258800 */'.
Renamed generated local labels from offset-based names such as .L_00000020, .L_00000150, and .L_000003EC to address-based labels such as .L_8025BC40, .L_8025BD70, and .L_8025C00C.
Preserved existing global function names visible in the excerpt, including mnCharSel_8025BC20 and mnCharSel_8025BD30.

Regenerated relocation operands so instructions reference concrete symbol addends and branch distances, for example HSD_SisLib_804D1124@ha emitted with lis r5, 0x804D-style high bits, mnCharSel_804D6CB0@sda21 with its sda21 load, and branch calls to HSD_SisLib_803A6530/lbLang_IsSavedLanguageJP/gmMainLib_8015CC34 with non-placeholder displacements.
smart_moves: Kept the change scoped to a single generated assembly file, which makes the large +12504/-12521 diff easier to interpret as a regeneration rather than a hand-edited logic change.
Maintained existing global symbol names while normalizing only generated labels and address comments, preserving cross-file references useful to ongoing decomp work.
Using address-based .L_802xxxxx labels makes future cross-referencing with map files, objdump output, and decompiled function addresses easier than zero-based .L_000xxxxx labels.
decomp_lessons: For undecompiled assembly files, periodically regenerating the asm with current address conventions can reduce friction before attempting C migration or symbol cleanup.

Large assembly regen PRs should be reviewed primarily for mechanical consistency: file range, instruction comments, local label targets, branch/call displacements, and symbol relocations should all move together.
When preserving generated assembly as a matching fallback, address-based labels can make decomp notes and future function extraction more searchable.

This PR provides no body or review discussion, so there is no direct evidence of a matching bug or hand-authored behavioral fix; treat it as infrastructure/synchronization work rather than a logic correction.
naming_conventions: Global menu character-select symbols use the module prefix and address suffix pattern 'mnCharSel_8025BC20' and 'mnCharSel_8025BD30'.
Generated local labels after regeneration use runtime address form '.L_8025BC40' rather than section-offset form '.L_00000020'.
External symbols shown in the excerpt retain subsystem prefixes such as HSD_SisLib_804D1124, HSD_SisLib_803A6530, HSD_SisLib_803A660C, lbLang_IsSavedLanguageJP, and gmMainLib_8015CC34.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Regenerate asm comments and labels from the correct text start address so local branches and labels line up with actual DOL addresses.
Prefer address-stable local labels in generated asm when the file is still used for matching or decomp navigation.
Check that relocation syntax remains symbolic where appropriate, such as HSD_SisLib_804D1124@ha/@l and mnCharSel_804D6CB0@sda21, while emitted instruction comments reflect resolved addresses.
A pure asm regen may show nearly equal additions and deletions; compare semantic instruction mnemonics around functions before assuming behavior changed.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile or further split asm/melee/mn/mncharsel.s.
Search for other 'Regen' PRs by r-burns that converted .L_000xxxxx labels to .L_802xxxxx labels.
Check whether subsequent mnCharSel C work reused the global symbol names mnCharSel_8025BC20 and mnCharSel_8025BD30.
